issue_title: hyphenated parameters
issue_body:
While giving a presentation on gabbi, I noticed that the use of underscores in request/response parameters seems somewhat inconsistent:

```yaml
tests:

- name: ...
  GET: /

  response_headers:
      Content-Type: text/html
```

For HTTP headers¹ the convention is to use hyphens while gabbi parameters use underscores. For the pedantically inclined, that's an unpleasant clash.

I realize it might be too late to change this now, unless we're willing to magically accept both. Either way, it seemed worth mentioning for posterity.


¹ arguably, to a lesser extent, also for JSON keys

issue_title: Drop python2.6 support
issue_body:
Hi folks,

firstly, since python2.6 (2.6.9) support has ended a long time ago [oct/2013](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/python-dev/2013-September/128287.html), I think it is time to move forward here too! 

Also, since `pydocstyle` has recently [dropped the `Python2.6` support](https://github.com/PyCQA/pydocstyle/commit/124105731030db48be870319458c1130634d726c#diff-22011eed535a779a25f5c419a6c12363), I think that `pylama` may go the same way and drop it too in order to keep using the most updated `pydocstyle` [2.0.0 at this moment].

My PR (#97) that fixes (#96) to add `pydocstyle2.0.0` support needs this too, since it will not pass on `python2.6` tests (because of `pydocstyle`).

Cheers! =)

issue_title: Simple prepending of "sudo" is not always helps
issue_body:
```bash
user@machine:/$ mkdir /var/www
mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/var/www’: Permission denied
user@machine:/$ fuck
sudo mkdir /var/www [enter/↑/↓/ctrl+c]
```
root is owner of /var/www

```bash
user@machine:/$ touch /var/www/1/2/3
touch: cannot touch ‘/var/www/1/2/3’: No such file or directory
user@machine:/$ fuck
mkdir -p /var/www/1/2 && touch /var/www/1/2/3 [enter/↑/↓/ctrl+c]
```

the command is ok, we have permission error
```bash
mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/var/www/1’: Permission denied
user@machine:/$ fuck
sudo mkdir -p /var/www/1/2 && touch /var/www/1/2/3 [enter/↑/↓/ctrl+c]
touch: cannot touch ‘/var/www/1/2/3’: Permission denied
```

permission error related to touch, but accroding to common pattern ("sudo + command") we have double sudo

```bash
user@machine:/$ fuck
sudo sudo mkdir -p /var/www/1/2 && touch /var/www/1/2/3 [enter/↑/↓/ctrl+c]
touch: cannot touch ‘/var/www/1/2/3’: Permission denied
```

issue_title: six.wraps doesn't ignore non-existing attributes like Python 3 does
issue_body:
Here's a repro script that fails in Python 2, but works in Python 3:
```python
import six
class Hello(object):
    def __call__(self):
        return "Hello"
f = Functor()
@six.wraps(f)
def world():
    return f() + ", world"
```

On Python 2 it fails with 
```
AttributeError: 'Functor' object has no attribute '__name__'
```
On Python 3 it succeeeds, because it was fixed in in https://bugs.python.org/issue3445. Given the trivial implementation it should be enough to backport the [update_wrapper](https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/445f1b35ce8461268438c8a6b327ddc764287e05/Lib/functools.py#L37-L65) function as-is.

issue_title: Extensions ignored in YIN format
issue_body:
```
If a module in *YIN format* is loaded via Context::search_module, then all YANG 
extensions appearing in it are ignored. It is demonstrated in the attached 
example:

1. Go to the "yang" directory first, everything works:

$ pyang test.yang
$ pyang -L hello.xml

2. In the "yin" directory, the first way also works but the other fails:

$ pyang test.yin  
$ pyang -L hello.xml
test.yin:7: warning: imported module ietf-yang-metadata not used
test.yin:10: error: no module with the namespace 
"urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:yang:ietf-yang-metadata" is imported

It is due to the fact the YinParser in Context::_ensure_revs is
initialised with the "extra" parameter containing 'no_extensions':True
For YangParser, no such parameter is present.
```

Original issue reported on code.google.com by `lada.lho...@gmail.com` on 27 Feb 2015 at 2:29

Attachments:
- [annot.tar.gz](https://storage.googleapis.com/google-code-attachments/pyang/issue-126/comment-0/annot.tar.gz)

issue_title: Backends must not reorder source files
issue_body:
Currently the Vivado backends (and others as well from a quick look) don't place HDL sources into the project file in the order they are resolved from the dependency resolver, but reorder the files. The Vivado backend, for example, groups the HDL files by file type (first all verilog files, then all sv files, then all vhdl files).

For most Verilog and VHDL files, the compile order doesn't matter.  However, sometimes it does matter -- for example, for package definitions in SystemVerilog. The SV standard requires that packages are defined before they are used, hence these files must be compiled first.

Note: Vivado doesn't seem to care about that rule and still compiles the resulting project without errors. However, SpyGlass isn't as lenient and fails the lint process. However, let's make sure we don't rely on lenient behavior by tools but do the right thing (TM).

TODOs:
- [ ] create testcases which check this behavior
- [ ] fix all backends showing to be problematic. Vivado is one of them.

issue_title: Missing support for atomic test and set operations
issue_body:
From reading the available docs, I cannot find out how to replace the
following python-ldap script fragment:
```
	c = ldap.open('ldap.example.com')
	DN = 'cn=objectcn,dc=ldap,dc=example,dc=com'
	ATT = 'cn'
	OVAL = 'testValue'
	NVAL = 'setValue'
	c.modify_s(DN, [(ldap.MOD_ADD, ATT, OVAL),
			(ldap.MOD_DELETE, ATT, NVAL)])
```
which would translate to the following LDIF atomic change operation:
```
	dn: cn=objectcn,dc=ldap,dc=example,dc=com
	changetype: modify
	delete: cn
	cn: testValue
	-
	add: cn
	cn: setValue
```
While the change operations are sequenced, the modify change will happen atomically on the server.

For the use of the operation as a locking primitive, see:
 - http://www.openldap.org/lists/openldap-software/200009/msg00559.html (K. Zeilenga answers to a question about locking)
 - http://ldapwiki.willeke.com/wiki/LDIF%20Atomic%20Operations (LDIF Atomic Operations article from ldapwiki site)

issue_title: Is there a bidirectional promoter feature?
issue_body:
Hi Ryan,

I  am looking into thee possibility of using gffutils to select genes from an encode gff that have bidirectional promoters. I noticed that the old (old) version of gffutils had this [functionality](https://github.com/daler/GFFutils_old/blob/8e032cdcfe83dbc1ec85e55cbf6bcc72a0059328/README.rst), but it appears to have been dropped. Cheers

issue_title: [Question] Finding a vector valued regression model
issue_body:
I have data samples in R^4 (an rgb color and a distance) and I want to use them to build a regression model to match observations in R^3 (an rgb color).

Is there a way in dlib, directly or indirectly, to build a model that takes values from R^4 and produces values in R^3 based upon my data? All of the regression examples for the various methods in dlib show scalar dependent variables and I'm not sure where and if this is a strict requirement.

I have seen some earlier responses on the sourceforge page from some years ago mention something about using empirical_kernel_map in response to a question similar to mine but I don't really understand the advice, that is it isn't obvious how it helps me obtain three scalars per input from the trained model.

Thanks

issue_title: Use installed dlib when compiling python extension.
issue_body:
Is there any reason to statically compile-in whole dlib into python extension library? I'm not against compiling stuff twice, but when I compile python extension and there is already dlib installed, the extension picks up config.h header of the installed one. This leads to inconsistency with options handling.

For instance, if dlib was compiled with PNG support, and the extension is being compiled without it, the PNG feature would be enabled anyways, because config.h installed by dlib has DLIB_PNG_SUPPORT defined.

issue_title: Python batch processing face detection/recognition run out of memory on GPU
issue_body:
I'm processing a large quantity of images. After about 160 images (~120 MB) the GPU ran out of memory.

Here how I'm  doing it:

````
import dlib
from scipy.misc import imread
import numpy as np

cnn_face_detector = dlib.cnn_face_detection_model_v1('mmod_human_face_detector.dat')
pose_predictor = dlib.shape_predictor('shape_predictor_68_face_landmarks.dat')
face_encoder = dlib.face_recognition_model_v1('dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat')

def faces_extraction(image):
    img = imread(image) 
    rect = cnn_face_detector(img)
    pose = dlib.full_object_detections()
    for r in rect:
        pose.append(pose_predictor(img, r.rect))
    encodings = face_encoder.compute_face_descriptor(img, pose)

    return [{'rect': (r.rect.left(), r.rect.top(), r.rect.right(), r.rect.bottom()),
                   'conf': r.confidence,
                   'landmark': [(point.x, point.y) for point in p.parts()],
                   'encoding': np.array(e)
            } for r, p, e in zip(rect, pose, encodings)]

for feature in features[:500]:
    feature['faces'] = faces_extraction(os.path.join(base_dir, feature['path']))

issue_title: Fallback scenario is ugly
issue_body:
In #117 and specifically in https://github.com/jaraco/keyring/issues/117#issuecomment-171330756, the assertion was made that

> @rsyring: Getting a None returned is probably never going to be the actual desired behavior.

But as reported in [bitbucket issue migration 60](https://github.com/jeffwidman/bitbucket-issue-migration/issues/60#issuecomment-174611323), that's not the case for libraries that optionally include keyring support but fallback to prompting for a password when none is found in the keyring ([bitbucket_issue_migration](https://github.com/jeffwidman/bitbucket-issue-migration/blob/master/migrate.py#L103-L104), ...) and almost certainly others.

I'm thinking we should change the fail backend to only error on set/delete.

issue_title: New CryptedFileKeyring doesn't follow keyring protocol
issue_body:
If I understand correctly (and please correct me if not), the new CryptedFileKeyring does not follow the keyring protocol, but instead attempts to implement a similar protocol. Rather than implementing the abstract methods of the BasicFileKeyring, it encrypts and decrypts the entire file, bypassing the machinery implemented in the parent class.

At the very least, this causes the tests to fail (when attempting to call encrypt and decrypt).

It may be the case that this implementation is sound as simply needs to not subclass from BasicFileKeyring and have the tests updated accordingly.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/kang/python-keyring-lib/issue/64
- Originally reported by: Jason R. Coombs
- Originally created at: 2012-06-09T20:30:11.103

issue_title: OSX install broken when using virtualenvs?
issue_body:
This lib seemed way interesting and got a bit of publicity a few weeks ago, so I decided to check it out the usual way: create a virtualenv, pip install the library inside it and play around.

Except I didn't get anywhere: `get_password` would always return `None` (with no keychain prompt) and `set_password` would throw `OSError: Can't store password in Keychain`, in both cases whatever the service & account are. After a few reinstallations, I tried compiling from scratch (cloning from mercurial and `python setup.py build`… success. Both tip (4a551eda6bf8) and tag 0.2 build and work perfectly.

Try to `python setup.py install` it in my virtualenv, failure again (except it now throws `keyring.backend.PasswordSetError` on `set_password`, due to being tip I guess).

Tested the same scenario in a fresh OpenSUSE image, no issue.

issue_title: Events stops triggering rules after random number of events get dispatched
issue_body:
Hi,

I recently run into this problem that new events stop triggering rules after random number of events get dispatched. 

Here is my ruleset definitions:
```python
with ruleset('daware'):
    @when_all(pri(1),
              c.first << +s.last,
              c.second << (m.id == c.first.last + 1) & (m.action == 'open'))
    def daware_fopen(c):
        c.s.last = c.second.id
        logging.error('daware_fopen get message id {0}'.format(c.second.id))

    @when_all(pri(1),
              c.first << +s.last,
              c.second << (m.id == c.first.last + 1) & (m.action == 'write'))
    def daware_write_access(c):
        c.s.last = c.second.id
        logging.error('daware_write_access get message id {0}'.format(c.second.id))

    @when_all(pri(1),
              c.first << +s.last,
              c.second << (m.id == c.first.last + 1) & (m.action == 'read'))
    def daware_read_access(c):
        c.s.last = c.second.id
        logging.error('daware_read_access get message id {0}'.format(c.second.id))

    @when_all(pri(2), m.id > 0)
    def fallback(c):
        logging.error('fallback id {0}'.format(c.m.id))

    @when_start
    def start_test(host):
        host.patch_state('daware', {'sid': 1, 'last': 0})
```
Basically, I just log every event into a log file with python **logging** package. Since I want these events being logged in the same sequence as the event id, I take your advice from another post to store the last processed id in the host context. 

I have another daemon running locally which sends events by **HTTP POST** around 30k events in 30 seconds to local address http://127.0.0.1/data_aware/1.

I add a line in **Application** class to dump all the received messages to my log file, so I can confirm that all the events I post from my daemon has been received by durable_rules. 

The output log will be like this 
```
ERROR:root:daware_write_access get message id 6684
ERROR:root:daware_write_access get message id 6685
INFO:root:Get POST: {u'sid': u'1', u'action': u'write', u'id': 6882}
INFO:werkzeug:127.0.0.1 - - [29/Aug/2016 17:48:45] "POST /data_aware/1 HTTP/1.1" 200 -
```
As you can see that 6685 is the last event id that my ruleset had logged, but the werkzeug package had logged an POST with id = 6882.

This issue cannot be consistently reproduced in every around of my experiment, but I can always reproduce this in 10 rounds which means that 300k events had been sent.

This looks like a bug to me, but I cannot really confirm it. Can you think of anything that could lead to this behaviour or point me some directions to help me clarify this problem?

If you need more information or if there is anything I can help, please feel free to let me know.

I have noticed a couple of issues when building the documentation for the package. I have prepared Pull Requests to fix some of them, and I would appreciate if you could take a look/comment on/merge them.

1) `ImportErrror` traceback:
```
WARNING: [autosummary] failed to import u'pynlpl.formats.folia.AlignmentReference': no module named pynlpl.formats.folia.AlignmentReference
```
This happens because the class is actually named `AlignReference` (https://github.com/proycon/pynlpl/blob/master/formats/folia.py#L4385)

issue_title: Unable to express list of maps as a literal in variables
*/int syntax doesn't appear to work
Too-strict version check causes problems for wheels
issue_body:
A typical use case for `capnpy` is the following chains of requirements (`lib`, `app` are Python packages):

```
capnpy (from app)
capnpy (from lib->app)
```

i.e. `lib` contains a `.capnproto` schema (and so has `capnpy` as a **compile-time** requirement) and `app` makes use of the compiled schema provided by `lib` (and so has both `capnpy` _and_ `lib` as **run-time** requirements).

Schema compilation can be slow, so it is desirable to publish a wheel for `lib`. If so, then compile-time != run-time in general, and the versions of `capnpy` used by `lib` and `app` may be different, but `util.check_version` insists that these versions be identical. This means that new releases of `capnpy` will break the existing wheel(s) for `lib`, and require a new versions to be built and published with the latest `capnpy`.

Pinning the `capnpy` version in `lib` unfortunately is not a solution, as `pip` is too dumb to notice the conflict; it first installs the unconstrained version requested by `app`, and later happily reports "Requirement already satisfied" when being asked to install the pinned version required by `lib`. A possible workaround would be to omit `capnpy` from the requirements of `app` altogether, and rely on the `lib` requirement to bring in the pinned version, but this seems like bad practice.

Possible solutions I can see:

1. Provide compatibility (between minor releases), such that `app` and `lib` can both specify `capnpy<N+1.0.0`. This really just reduces the frequency of breakages, but may be good enough.

2. Bundle the "incompatible" parts of the `capnpy` run-time library into wheels at compile-time

issue_title: Cannot read an unsorted file
issue_body:
Hi Aymeric,

Hats off to your work on this mdfreader!

Now, I would like to let you know that your module doesn't work for an unsorted file.
If you want to read every channel in an unsorted file, you can do it recursively based on the same way you are reading channel group (CG block).
If a channel (CN block) is of type 8 (if I correctly remember) then it's a structure channel, it means it contains sub-channels (child CN block), you can view it as the same as a channel group (CG block). There is a section about structure channels on this page: https://wiki.asam.net/display/STANDARDS/ASAM+MDF

I wish I could submit myself a PR to solve this issue but I lack understandings of your code to do it yet. If I find a way, I may help you. Otherwise, I hope you can figure out a fix yourself.

Cheers, Julien.

issue_title: Bash sequences in suckless terminal (st)
issue_body:
Using suckless/simple terminal (st) version 0.8.1 and pywal version 3.1.0. 

Instead of compiling the colors into the terminal I rely on the bash sequences to color the terminal. But suckless terminal has an issue with this because it does not seem to pick the colors for the cursor, even though it's picking the other colors when using the line `(cat ~/.cache/wal/sequences &)` in .bashrc.

I was able to fix this by editing the file `~/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pywal/sequences.py` by adding a comma on line number 57 and adding this line after that:

    set_color(256, colors["special"]["foreground"])

So now line 58 looks just like line 57 but with 256 instead of 232 and without the comma at the end that you just added to line 57. The bash sequences are now picked up correctly by the suckless terminal and the cursor color is right.

issue_title: (Linux) psutil.pids() not showing all processes
issue_body:
The test case:

```
    def test_pid_exists_range(self):
        reap_children()
        pids = set(psutil.pids())
        for pid in range(max(pids) + 1):
            if pid not in pids:
                try:
                    assert not psutil.pid_exists(pid)
                except AssertionError:
                    assert not os.path.exists('/proc/%s' % pid)
```

This fails with:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/giampaolo/svn/psutil/test/test_psutil.py", line 738, in test_pid_exists_range
    assert not os.path.exists('/proc/%s' % pid), pid
AssertionError: 947
```

...and happens both as root and an unprivileged user.
For some reason it seems `os.listdir('/proc')` won't list directory '947' but `os.stat('947')` won't fail (directory exists) and hence also `psutil.Process(947)` and `psutil.pid_exists(947)` succeed.
`ls /proc` also won't show directory 947 but `ls /proc/947` succeeds.
This is weird.

issue_title: Do not log passwords when using PLAIN SASL authentication and with enabled DEBUG logs by default
issue_body:
This will be tricky, since DEBUG logs by default contain a full dump of all XML going over the XMLStream, and the XMLStream does not know about sensitivity of data.

One approach would be to add a context-manager/flag to XMLStream to suppress all DEBUG logging. This needs to be overridable to debug issues during authentication.

The WSGI specification says, it must be a string (https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-3333/#environ-variables). This prevents running application wrapped with `wsgiref.validate.validator`.

Traceback:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/wsgiref/validate.py", line 153, in lint_app
    check_environ(environ)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/wsgiref/validate.py", line 328, in check_environ
    % (key, type(environ[key]), environ[key]))
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/wsgiref/validate.py", line 128, in assert_
    raise AssertionError(*args)
AssertionError: Environmental variable SERVER_PORT is not a string: <class 'int'> (value: 8000)
```

Shall I fix it and write a pull request?

issue_title: Inline Images In Incorrect MIME Block
issue_body:
In the **_prepare_message** method, inline images are being correctly referenced using Content-ID (CID tag in HTML img, etc.).

However, the image itself is then attached to the "top-level" msg object (which would be correct for a regular attachment), but should be attached to the msg_related MIME part in order for clients to properly embed the image inline.  Some clients handle this more gracefully than others (Thurnderbird shows a "broken image" icon, for example).

Also, order of attachments matter ... so the msg_related.attach() of the htmlstr must happen *before* the msg_related.attach() of the content_object.

issue_title: pg8000 can't handle freezegun's FakeDate/FakeDateTime types (maybe not freezegun's problem?)
issue_body:
I'm not sure if this really an issue with `freezegun` or `pg8000`, or if I'm doing something stupid, but I can't use `freezegun`-generated dates for insertions or selections in a postgres database using the `pg8000` interface.  (This works OK with `psycopg2` instead of `pg8000` however.)

I first came across this while writing integration tests for a Flask app using `flask-sqlalchemy`, but I've boiled it down to a simple example involving only `freezegun` and `pg8000` as follows.

Given a postgres database called `foo` with one table `foo` with one date column:

```
$ psql -U postgres foo 
psql (9.6.1)
Type "help" for help.

foo=# \d foo
    Table "public.foo"
 Column | Type | Modifiers 
--------+------+-----------
 when   | date | 
```

the following connects to it via `pg8000` and attempts to insert a row inside a `freezegun` context:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python

import datetime

from freezegun import freeze_time
import pg8000

conn = pg8000.connect(user='postgres', database='foo')
cursor = conn.cursor()
with freeze_time('2016-12-25'):
    today = datetime.date.today()
cursor.execute('INSERT INTO foo (when) VALUES (%s)', (today,))
```

and it fails with the following traceback:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/gimbo/.virtualenvs/sqlwtf/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pg8000/core.py", line 1849, in make_params
    params.append(self.py_types[typ])
KeyError: <class 'freezegun.api.FakeDate'>

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/gimbo/.virtualenvs/sqlwtf/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pg8000/core.py", line 1852, in make_params
    params.append(self.inspect_funcs[typ](value))
KeyError: <class 'freezegun.api.FakeDate'>

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./wtf8.py", line 12, in <module>
    cursor.execute('INSERT INTO foo (when) VALUES (%s)', (today,))
  File "/home/gimbo/.virtualenvs/sqlwtf/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pg8000/core.py", line 906, in execute
    self._c.execute(self, operation, args)
  File "/home/gimbo/.virtualenvs/sqlwtf/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pg8000/core.py", line 1887, in execute
    params = self.make_params(args)
  File "/home/gimbo/.virtualenvs/sqlwtf/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pg8000/core.py", line 1855, in make_params
    "type " + str(e) + "not mapped to pg type")
pg8000.core.NotSupportedError: type <class 'freezegun.api.FakeDate'>not mapped to pg type
```

If I use `psycopg2` rather than `pg8000` it works OK, so maybe the problem is not really `freezegun`'s; OTOH maybe not, so I thought I'd raise it.

As a side note: I tried to get around this by having `freezegun` generate the frozen `FakeDate` and then constructing a "real" `datetime.date` from it piece by piece, but that fails because under `freezegun`, the `datetime.date()` constructor *always* returns a `FakeDate` - which goes beyond what I thought `freezegun` would do from reading its `README`.  That means I can never create an actual `datetime.date` while `freezegun` is in play, which seems problematic in itself to me.  For example:

```
ld0 $ python                                                   
Python 3.4.5 (default, Dec  1 2016, 20:09:55) 
[GCC 6.2.1 20160830] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
Python shell history and tab completion are enabled.
>>> import datetime
>>> import freezegun
>>> with freezegun.freeze_time('2016-12-25'):
...     foo = datetime.date(2017, 1, 3)
...     bar = datetime.datetime(2017, 1, 3, 16, 10, 30)
... 
>>> foo
FakeDate(2017, 1, 3)
>>> bar
FakeDatetime(2017, 1, 3, 16, 10, 30)
```

issue_title: "Approximation" algorithm
issue_body:
The "approximation" algorithm is poorly named and the implementation doesn't make sense to me.

The word approximation means an estimate or "not exact". Generally an approximation is used when the algorithm is faster or requires less memory. The trade off is less accuracy. Based on what the functions are doing, the code is "interpolating". Interpolation tries to improve accuracy by building a model of multiple points and calculating the most likely value. Approximation and Interpolation are basically opposites in this context. I suggest the public interface is changed to use "Interpolate" instead.

In regards to implementation, the function "approximation" appears to be using a linear distance weighted function. However, it doesn't use the closest value to the point of interest. Currently, if the point lies in the center cell of a 3x3 grid:
```
---
-+-
---
```
The cells used to compute the elevations and the weights are as follows:
```
-*-
*-*
-*-
```
The center cell is not checked. This is illogical since it contains the closest point. Also, the weighting function does not appear to be any standard GIS method

I suggest the library offer 3 spatial interpolation methods: Nearest Neighbor, Natural Neighbor, Inverse Distance Weighted.

Nearest Neighbor would return the elevation of the cell that contains the point, same as the current implementation using "Approximate=False"
Natural Neighbor would use the four closest points and weighting them based on the new areas of a voronoi tesselation: [Natural Neighbor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natural_neighbor_interpolation)
IDW includes all point in some radius and weighted by 1/distance. This could be offered by "IDW5"
```
-*-
***
-*-
```

and "IDW13"
```
--*--
-***-
*****
-***-
--*--
```

Thoughts?

issue_title: Ignores IN_Q_OVERFLOW events
issue_body:
We are using this PyInotify on large directory trees holding millions of files with the possibility of large batches of file additions or deletions at any time. By keeping the kernel-level queue size large enough we generally avoid IN_Q_OVERFLOW events from the kernel. However, we've been surprised a few times and in those cases we lose file events with no warning.

It would be good to add explicit handling of the IN_Q_OVERFLOW event. In 0.2.7 if the kernel generates IN_Q_OVERFLOW, I believe that __handle_inotify_event immediately returns, event_gen's loop terminates, and then yields a final None event without explanation.

I haven't tried 0.2.8 yet, but it appears that the IN_Q_OVERFLOW event is simply ignored and operation continues. Assuming this is the case, this is a step backward from the standpoint of IN_Q_OVERFLOW.

I can work on this, but would like to get advice on what to do when it occurs. Options:

- yield an event with no path, wd=-1, and the IN_Q_OVERFLOW mask
- raise an exception

What do you think?

Thanks, Kim

issue_title: Add a `replace` method to an expression
issue_body:
This should have the same semantics as the [stdlib](https://docs.python.org/2.7/library/string.html?highlight=string%20replace#string.replace) `string.replace()`:

```
string.replace(s, old, new[, maxreplace])

    Return a copy of string s with all occurrences of substring old replaced by new. 
    If the optional argument maxreplace is given, the first maxreplace occurrences are replaced.
```

 
This is more or less what `subs()` does but subs is too complex for my case and I do not need recursivity. 

I need a simpler lower level way to do only one replacement at a time and not many. There are a few wrinkles in the subs code too where iterating on `items()` could be replaced by a cleaner and more efficient `get()` that could be cleaned up too.

LookupError: Invalid tag (0008, 0050): unknown encoding: ISO 2022 IR 87

because it does not recognize ISO 2022 IR 6 as an encoding.

issue_title: Well-behaved subclasses for python builtins
issue_body:
See https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pydicom/TdvZxrdK4QQ.  Eli made a good point there that any subclasses of standard python objects should only add behavior, not modify the base class behavior.  E.g. a "reflexive" test like:
```code=python
if isinstance(s, str): 
    assert s == str(s) 
```
should work.

Two examples where it does not work currently are in the `__str__` of the UID class, and in floats where the number is quoted.

issue_title: Multiple trailing spaces for elements with VR 'AE' not stripped
issue_body:
```python
>>> from pydicom.dataset import Dataset
>>> from pydicom.filebase import DicomBytesIO
>>> from pydicom.filereader import read_dataset
>>> from pydicom.filewriter import write_dataset
>>> ds = Dataset()
>>> ds.DestinationAE = 'TESTTEST10      ' # 16 chars
>>> ds
(2100, 0140) Destination AE                      AE: 'TESTTEST10      '
>>> fp = DicomBytesIO()
>>> fp.is_implicit_VR = True
>>> fp.is_little_endian = True
>>> write_dataset(fp, ds)
>>> fp.parent.seek(0)
>>> out = read_dataset(fp.parent, True, True)
>>> out # 15 chars
(2100, 0140) Destination AE                      AE: 'TESTTEST10     '
```
PS3.5 6.2 says trailing spaces are non-significant, they should be stripped out during the read.

issue_title: DataElements pretending they are Sequences?
issue_body:
If access a Sequence in a Dataset in two ways, one gets back either a Sequence or a DataElement, which pretends to be a Sequence:

```
import dicom
import os

# load one of the test plans
folder = os.path.join(os.path.split(dicom.__file__)[0], "testfiles")
path = os.path.join(folder, "rtplan.dcm")
plan = dicom.read_file(path)

s1 = plan.DoseReferenceSequence
s2 = plan[0x300a, 0x0010] # DoseReferenceSequence Tag
print(repr(s1), type(s1))
print(repr(s2), type(s2))
```
```
<Sequence, length 2, at 7F6AE823AEA8> <class 'dicom.sequence.Sequence'>
<Sequence, length 2, at 7F6AE823AEA8> <class 'dicom.dataelem.DataElement'>
```

Is this considered to be a bug? If not what is the recommended way to find out, whether a DataElement object is really a DataElement or a Sequence in disguise?

issue_title: Error in Class Activation map for hidden layers
issue_body:
I was trying out the class activation map of some cat and dog images for different layers of VGG16 (using `keras.applications.vgg16`, not `vis.utils.vggnet`), and the code, shown in (https://raghakot.github.io/keras-vis/visualizations/attention/) does not seem to be working for layers other than 'predictions'. When I use `layer_name='block1_conv2'`, it is throwing an error, `ValueError: Unable to detect penultimate 'Convolution2D' or 'Pooling2D' layer for layer_idx: 2`. On the other hand, for `layer_name='block2_conv2'`, or `layer_name='block3_conv2'` there is an `AssertionError` for `len(item_slice) == 4`.

Further, when I am using `layer_name='predictions'`, the prediction is performed correctly for the cat and dog image (Tabby, and Siberian Husky), but the heat map is barely on the dog.

![keras-vis-cam-2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11420071/27305801-242d7e04-5511-11e7-9d6b-c35a0023087c.jpeg)

The problem is seen even for the images shown in the website, at least for the Tiger and the Spider Web.

![keras-vis-cam-original](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11420071/27307537-2a7a1d4c-5518-11e7-9d51-c206e0a617b4.jpeg)

When I use the VGG16 from `vis.utils.vggnet`, though, the `heatmap` makes sense!

![keras-vis-cam](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11420071/27307196-95d6cef2-5516-11e7-955e-da718123b42f.jpeg)
![keras-vis-cam-original](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11420071/27342320-dcbe8af2-55ad-11e7-9216-29e1adc2752e.jpeg)


Though, even in this case, when I change the layer to `fc1` or `fc2`, the entirety of the image lights up.

![keras-vis-cam-original-2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11420071/27342035-fc9b31f0-55ac-11e7-93cf-06068ed78c29.jpeg)
And the same error as mentioned before shows up when I try see the map for intermediate Convolution layers. How can this be solved?

The code I am using is:
```

import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from keras.preprocessing.image import img_to_array

# Build the VGG16 network with ImageNet weights

# Images corresponding to cat, and dog

    "cat.jpg",
    "dog.jpg"
]

heatmaps = []
for path in image_paths:
    seed_img = utils.load_img(path, target_size=(224, 224))
    x = np.expand_dims(img_to_array(seed_img), axis=0)
    x = preprocess_input(x)
    pred_class = np.argmax(model.predict(x))
    print('Predicted class is ' +str(decode_predictions(model.predict(x), top=1)[0][0]))
    # Here we are asking it to show attention such that prob of `pred_class` is maximized.
    heatmap = visualize_cam(model, layer_idx, [pred_class], seed_img)
    heatmaps.append(heatmap)

plt.imshow(utils.stitch_images(heatmaps))
plt.axis('off')
plt.title('Class Activation map')
plt.savefig('keras-vis-cam-2.jpeg')
```

issue_title: Text hidden from message area
issue_body:
Specifically, I'm referring to `button_box.py`, but this issue is probably present in any box that uses a similar messageArea.

When lines in a message are too long, text is hidden out of the message area. This seems to be a result of text-wrapping creates additional "lines" (or rather the line's height is larger than 1 row) that are not counted by the `get_num_lines` method, because word-wraps don't count as an additional index.

You can see in the following image that the lines that print the SHA384 and SHA512 hashes are so long that they are wrapped. As a result, the bottom two lines of the text are not visible. It is possible to scroll down the messageArea, however there is no scrollbar or otherwise any indication to a user that more text is present.

![Image of issue](http://i.imgur.com/ETPx0U7.jpg)

To remedy this issue, I recommend addressing either (1) How the height of the messageArea is set, or (2) add a scrollbar to the messageArea.

I have a working patch that addresses (1) by checking line lengths against the messageArea width. I can submit a pull request, if that's an amenable solution, after I do some more testing.

import pyautogui

sleep (1)

```

[SeatDefaults]

issue_title: Reading duplicate note-off events results in zero-duration notes
issue_body:
When the input MIDI file contains note-off events for already inactive notes (with note-off events given before) pretty-midi still creates note events with zero duration. Writing such notes then results in spurious long-running notes that were not present originally.

Example: [bach-js_kunst_der_fuge_01_(c)unknown[2].mid](https://filebin.ca/3ThfIaDkkjvD/bach-js_kunst_der_fuge_01_cunknown2.mid) (source: kunstderfuge.com)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/446124/28301674-a643cc56-6bb2-11e7-87df-517e0f4f7858.png)

And written one:

issue_title: Refactoring
issue_body:
Hello! Thank you for the library, it became very handy and I enjoyed using it.
What do you think about little refactoring of the code I've seen while using your library:

I didn't enjoy to re-instantiate the `Client`s everytime I've got an **access token**. That's why I fixed this. Also, after refactoring I am able to create instances in `aioauth_client.py` file (for example) and import instances to my project like: `from aioauth_client import facebook_client()`. Would you like me to **PR**?

**OPTIONAL**: the API you're using for requests here is outdated, so I believe I could refactor it from 
`response = yield from self.request('POST', self.access_token_url, data=payload, loop=loop)` to         ```
async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
            async with session.post(self.access_token_url, data=payload) as resp:
``` syntax and use `async/await` syntax instead of old one, but it'll break compatibility with older Python versions. On the other hand,  `aiohttp`s API is changing frequently so maybe we should move on to be up-to-date with them?

issue_title: Documentation for `-p` flag is hard to find
issue_body:
Here's an excerpt from the output of `nvr --help`:

    --remote-tab [<file> [<file> ...]], -p [<file> [<file> ...]]
                          Like --remote, but use :tabedit.

    ...other flags...

    -o <file> [<file> ...]
                          Open files via ":split".
    -O <file> [<file> ...]
                          Open files via ":vsplit".

I was curious to know if there was a `:tabedit` equivalent to the `-o` and `-O` flags. I eventually realised that the `-p` flag was what I was looking for, but it took me a while to find it!

I suggest that the `nvr --help` output should read as follows:

    --remote-tab [<file> [<file> ...]]
                          Like --remote, but use :tabedit.

    ...other flags...

    -o <file> [<file> ...]
                          Open files via ":split".
    -O <file> [<file> ...]
                          Open files via ":vsplit".
    -p <file> [<file> ...]
                          Open files via ":tabedit".

Making the `-p` flag much easier to find. Are there any technical reasons why the documentation doesn't read this way already?

issue_title: Mistletoe plant and logo
issue_body:
Hi,

first of all, thanks for the tool: it's a piece of cake for markdown parsing (and also for custom rendering)!

Just for the sake of precision, I'd like to point out a common misconception about mistletoe, which is not the plant depicted in the logo. The plant of the logo is either the [Ruscus aculeatus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/), also called *butcher's-broom* or *christmas berry* or the [Ilex aquifolium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilex_aquifolium), called *christmas holly*. Instead, the **mistletoe** is the [Viscum album](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mistletoe), which has white berries and plain thick leaves (not spiny as the other two plants).

I'd like also to make clear that letting you change the logo is not my purpose, but I want only to friendly make you aware of which is the *“right“* **mistletoe**. :-)

All the best,

Luca

issue_title: Usage, manipulating mouse input
issue_body:
Hi,

I was wondering if someone would be able to help me with a problem I am facing.

The code below is meant to intercept the mouse input and translate it by a factor of 2.
However, it stops working after intercepting the first two on_move mouse events and locks up the mouse.
I suspect it is something to do with concurrency and the listener but am unsure.
Any help would be appreciated :smile: 

``` python
from pynput import mouse


class PassingMouseListenerException(Exception): pass


class Mouse:
    def __init__(self):
        self.control_display_gain = 2
        self.last_position = None
        self.mouse = mouse.Controller()

    def on_move(self, x, y):
        if self.last_position is None:
            self.last_position = (x, y)
        else:
            x_difference = (x - self.last_position[0]) * self.control_display_gain
            y_difference = (y - self.last_position[1]) * self.control_display_gain
            pos = (x_difference + self.last_position[0], y_difference + self.last_position[1])
            print("difference: {0}, {1}".format(x_difference, y_difference))
            print("old position: {0}, {1} captured position: {2}, {3} new position: {4}, {5}".format(self.last_position[0], self.last_position[1], x, y, pos[0], pos[1]))
            self.mouse.move(x_difference, y_difference)
            self.last_position = pos

    def on_click(self, x, y, button, pressed):
        print("I clicked")

    def on_scroll(self, x, y, dx, dy):
        pass

    def run(self):
        self.last_position = self.mouse.position
        with mouse.Listener(
                on_move=self.on_move,
                on_click=self.on_click,
                on_scroll=self.on_scroll) as listener:

            try:
                listener.join()
            except Exception as e:
                print("listener died: {}".format(e))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    program = Mouse()
    program.run()
```

issue_title: Add CRC16 verification
issue_body:
Telegram values include a CRC16 checksum which can be used that the telegram is valid. This check prevents invalid data due to corrupted values, as the serial interface doesn't have any built-in verification.

It will raise a subtype of the existing ParseError from the serial client.

The CRC16 check currently verifies DSMR 4.2 telegrams. Telegrams of other versions are appreciated. I will anonymize them of equipment identifiers before adding them to the repository. If you decide to supply one, please leave them intact so i have valid data to test with before anonimizing them.

[bumpversion]
current_version = 1.1.4

[egg_info]

[easy_install]
index_url = http://myserver/pypi

[bumpversion:file:setup.py]
```

That comment is used by my CI tool -- on pull requests, it uncomments it to build a dev package. If bumpversion deletes it, it breaks the process. 

For now, I'm switching back to using `bumpversion.cfg`, but I was hoping to consolidate this in `setup.cfg`

issue_title: Using flake8 inflates junit.xml output significantly
issue_body:
**tl;dr** The `junit.xml` report generated when running with `--flake8` is about x130 (130 times) of the report size without `--flake8`.

When running `py.test` on a relatively large project without using `--flake8` option, the behavior is "ok". `py.test` collects about 160 "items" from a project of about 160 modules, and outputs a `junit.xml` report around 1.4MB in size.

In contrast, when running **with** `--flake8` option, on the same source project, `py.test` collects about 314 items and outputs a report around 190MB in size.

My environment is:

```
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.11, pytest-3.0.1, py-1.4.31, pluggy-0.3.1
django settings: my_project.test_settings (from environment variable)
rootdir: /Users/pavel/project_root, inifile: pytest.ini
plugins: cov-2.3.1, django-2.9.1, flake8-0.6, mock-1.2
```

My `pytest.ini` is:

```
[pytest]
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = my_project.test_settings
flake8-ignore =
    *.py E402 E126 E127 E265 E128 W503 E122 E731 E713 W191 E101 E226
    urls.py ALL
    **/migrations/** ALL
    **/tests/** E501
flake8-max-line-length = 110
addopts =
    --flake8
    --junitxml=reports/junit.xml
```

Seems that `pytest==2.9.2` does not result in the same behaviour. Specifically, if existing compiled pyc/cache files of the modules exist (from the pytest==3.0.1 run), many of the collected items are skipped, resulting in a small xml file.  However, if cache/pyc files are cleared, the tests report is again at 190MB. Running without `--flake8` again results in a 1.4MB xml.

issue_title: Filtering items with a regex
issue_body:
I currently have [a custom script](https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/blob/master/scripts/dev/run_vulture.py) to run vulture for two reasons:

- Dynamically generating a whitelist.
- Filtering any functions matching `[a-z]+[A-Z][a-zA-Z]+` because those are typically virtual methods provided by Qt/PyQt, i.e. from C++ - any Python methods are named `snake_case` in my project.

Unfortunately, the script broke quite often recently because things changed in vulture in subtle ways. I don't think the whitelist is the problem (after all, I just pass that to vulture), but the filtering means I need to have my own `report` there so I can filter the items before they are reported.

I say two ways around this:

- Adding something like a `--func-filter-regex` (and maybe `--props-filter-regex`, etc.) commandline argument.
- Adding something like a `filter_callback` to `vulture.report`, which is only intended for when using vulture programmatically, and gets called with each `Item` once. If it returns `True`, the given item does not get reported.

Both of those would probably make my script a bit less relying on vulture internals. For the first one, I could even switch to running `vulture.main`.

What do you think?

issue_title: possible issue with 0.7.10
issue_body:
I've upgraded to 0.7.10 to test the ngMessages work i've been doing but have come across an issue.

I have a base class that all my forms inherit from. It used to look like this for 0.7.3:
```
class BaseNgForm(NgMessagesMixin,
                 NgModelFormMixin,
                 NgFormValidationMixin,
                 Bootstrap3FormMixin):
        ...
```

After i'd updated to 0.7.10 i got this error

```AttributeError: 'EmailField' object has no attribute 'get_potential_errors'```

After a bit of poking around i could see that my fields weren't being converted to the djangular mixin fields and realised that in your example you use Bootstrap3Form as a base instead. So i changed to that:
```
class BaseNgForm(NgMessagesMixin,
                 NgModelFormMixin,
                 NgFormValidationMixin,
                 Bootstrap3Form):
```
But i'm still getting the same error.

I'm using all_auth and have their signin, signup and reset password forms all on the same page. Looking further into this i've found that the first form that gets processed is the reset form, which only has an EmailField. This get's processed ok by `NgFormValidationMixin.get_field_errors` as the `bound_field.field` is a `djangular.forms.angular_base.EmailField`.

However the next form it processes is signin, whose first field is also an `EmailField`. But even though this form also inherits from `BaseNgForm`, the `bound_field.field` is a `django.forms.fields.EmailField`.

I've checked through the example code to see if i'm missing something and have also read the docs, but can't see anything different. I've also tried to recreate this issue in the example by rendering 2 forms on the same page. But it doesn't occur.

Sorry for the small amount of info to work with, but i'm just wondering if there's anything that jumps out at you that might be causing this?

issue_title: Flaws of current `.travis.yml` test suite
issue_body:
Current test suite as defined in `travis.yml` and `Makefile` has couple of problems:

- tests only python 2.7 and 3.6
- difficult to run locally for multiple python versions
- `Makefile` require `make`, which is not always available under Windows making test setup more difficult
- use of `pipenv` brings problems:
  - test is slowed down
  - current `Pipfile`installs dependencies, which shall be installed with `records` package, effectively hiding possible problems in `setup.py` requirements (e.g. `setup.py` omitting important dependency, which shall be installed by it).
  - is is not clear, where gets `pytest` installed

Proposal for fix is:
- do not use `pipenv` for testing (`Pipfile.lock` is not used, requirements shall be defined by `setup.py` so `default` section in `Pipfile` shall be empty or only with `-e .`) Vulnerability to pypi python package updates can be considered as positive feature (detecting real life cases, which shall work well)
- refactor `.travis.yml` to `tox`

issue_body:
在提交前，请确保您已经检查了以下内容!

- [ ] 您可以在浏览器中登陆微信账号，但不能使用`itchat`登陆
- [ ] 我已经阅读并按[文档][document] 中的指引进行了操作
- [ ] 您的问题没有在[issues][issues]报告，否则请在原有issue下报告
- [ ] 本问题确实关于`itchat`, 而不是其他项目.
- [ ] 如果你的问题关于稳定性，建议尝试对网络稳定性要求极低的[itchatmp][itchatmp]项目

请使用`itchat.run(debug=True)`运行，并将输出粘贴在下面:

```
[在这里粘贴完整日志]
```

您的itchat版本为：`[在这里填写版本号]`。（可通过`python -c "import itchat;print(itchat.__version__)"`获取）

其他的内容或者问题更详细的描述都可以添加在下面：

> 最近在使用itchat做微信机器人，使用exitCallback进行退出回调处理时，发现exitCallback方法未完整执行。
目前分析，可能是接收到状态异常消息后，itchat.run方法结束，导致itchat的python主进程结束，此时maintainThread线程将同时被杀死，所以exitCallback方法未被正常执行，因run没做join处理。
目前准备直接导出itchat源码，打包到项目中使用。。提此issue看是否还有人在维护。。。

issue_title: verbatim should not be highlighted as code
issue_body:
The following C declaration ```cpp /** * @verbatim return abcd @endverbatim * * @param[out] dest Destination pointer. * @param[in] src Source pointer * @param n number of bytes */ void *memcpy(void *dest, const void *src, size_t n); ``` outputs the following XML ```xml return abcd ``` and if I look into the HTML output I get ```html 
return abcd
``` The documentation of `verbatim` says in [1] > Starts a block of text that will be verbatim included in the documentation. The block should end with >a \endverbatim command. All commands are disabled in a verbatim block. so I don't expect syntax highlightning in a verbatim block. A test repo is at [2]. [1]: http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/manual/commands.html#cmdverbatim [2]: https://github.com/t-b/breathe-doxygen-filter-test

issue_title: Improve move generation speed
issue_body:
S. Tannous [describes a method for faster move generation in languages like Python](https://github.com/stannous/shatranj/blob/master/avoiding-rotations-final.pdf) by avoiding rotated bitboards. It comes with a [complete implementation of a chess engine](https://github.com/stannous/shatranj).

This would be an excellent way to improve move generation speed in python-chess.

issue_title: List of board positions in polyglot opening book
issue_body:
From the polyglot opening book, I want to create a dictionary that maps a fen to the main entry in the book:

```python
import chess.polyglot
fen_to_best_move = dict()

with chess.polyglot.open_reader("data/polyglot/performance.bin") as book:
    for board in boards_in_polyglot_book:
        fen_to_best_move[board.fen()] = book.find(board)
```

From reading the docs and source, it isn't clear to me if there is an easy way to do this without exhaustively checking if boards are in the book. Let me know if there is a good way to do this.

Thanks!

issue_title: WeekdayPositionExpression: unexpected keyword argument 'option' and 'weekday'
issue_body:
The Python (2.5) report following error:
  File "C:\programy.win\Python25\lib\site-packages\apscheduler\triggers.py", lin
e 35, in compile_single
    
    #!python
        return compiler(**match.groupdict())
    TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'option'
    

Solution:
Original code with error:

    
    #!python
    
    class WeekdayPositionExpression(object):
        options = ['1st', '2nd', '3rd', '4th', '5th', 'last']
        value_re = re.compile(r'(?P<option>%s) +(?P<weekday>(?:\d+|\w+))'
                              % '|'.join(options), re.IGNORECASE)
    

Fixed line:

    
    #!python
        value_re = re.compile(r'(?P<**option_name**>%s) +(?P<weekday_name>(?:\d+|\w+))'
                              % '|'.join(options), re.IGNORECASE)
    

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/agronholm/apscheduler/issue/2
- Originally reported by: [skokan](http://bitbucket.org/skokan)
- Originally created at: 2010-05-10T12:36:45.112

issue_title: Remove key if one type, leave it if another?
issue_body:
Is there a way to remove a key from a dict if it is a specific type, leave it if it is another, and be invalid if it is any other type?

Basically I want:
```
{'my_str': basestring} -> {'my_str': basestring}
{'my_str': None} -> {}
{'my_str': int, list, etc.} -> Invalid
```

I'm trying to use `Requires()` but it doesn't seem to work right.

Things I've tried include:
```
Schema({'my_str': Any(Remove(None), basestring)}) 
Schema({Optional('my_str'): Any(Remove(None), basestring)})
Schema({Remove('my_str'): None, 'my_str': basestring}) 
Schema({'my_str': basestring, Remove('my_str'): None}) 
Schema({'my_str': basestring, Remove(basestring): None})
```

The closest I've gotten is:
```
Schema({v.Remove('my_str'): None}, extra=v.ALLOW_EXTRA)
```
But unfortunately this 1) doesn't provide a way to validate `my_str` is actually a `basestring`, and 2) allows all other extras which I don't want.

Thanks!

issue_title: InotifyObserver does not emit sub-events when directory is moved to outside of watched directory
issue_body:
Say I have
```
+ foo
|+ bar
 |+ abc.txt
```
where I have a recursive watch on _foo_ using the InotifyObserver. If I move _bar_ to somewhere else, I get a _DirDeletedEvent_ for _foo_ but no _FileDeletedEvent_ for _abc.txt_. 

This differs from the behavior for _rm_ and also for _mv_ within the watched directory and it also differs from the behavior of the PollingObserver and the FSEventsObserver.

I guess the solution is to add a _generate_sub_deleted_events_ function an call it from the InotifyObserver like it does with _generate_sub_created_events_ and _generate_sub_moved_events_.

issue_title: Handlers registered via ``once()`` only get unregistered after the handler ran

The typical use-case is "I want SAP fluxes" or "I want PDC fluxes". I think it might be more sensible to have this function reorganize the lcdict, perhaps bookkeeping the same things as currently tracked in lcdict['columns'], but with two separate sub-dicts: one for PDC, one for SAP.

issue_title: code reuse can hide snooping attack
issue_body:
If a wormhole code can be intercepted, and used to receive a file, wormhole send --code can immediately be run by the attacker, reusing the same code to send the same file. The legitimate receiving user then receives the file they expected, via the code they expected and there is little or no indication that the data was intercepted.

I tried this attack, and was surprised to see it works. The documentation implies such attacks don't work:
> PAKE effectively trades off interaction against offline attacks. The only way for a network attacker to learn the shared key is to perform a man-in-the-middle attack during the initial connection attempt, and to correctly guess the code being used by both sides.

But with this attack, the PAKE keys are not needed; it's enough to intercept the code as it goes by on the network.

It also seems possible for the attacker to not wait to receive the whole file, in order to get their wormhole send running faster. It looks like they could stream the file they received into that send with a little work.

Note that the attacker has to supply the code to receive the file before the code can be reused. Trying to reuse the code before seems to cause receive to hang. Not sure if this is intentional, but it blocks the attack.

Could the wormhole server amelorate this attack by not letting wormhole codes be reused for say, 1 day? Might risk running out of codes or open a new DOS mechanism. But this attack seems rather bad..

issue_title: Telemetry xbox vs pc ( specifically 'blueZoneCustomOptions' )
issue_body:
Hey, sorry, me again.

I got around to playing with telemetry data, and it always fails for me with xbox matches. This time I'm not sure what the fix could possibly be, but like my last issue I did my best to trace down the problem.

It appears to be within Telemetry/events.py

on `__init__`  at this part:
```Python
    elif k == "blueZoneCustomOptions":  # serialized json
        if len(v) > 2:  # more than just '[]'
            setattr(self, camel_to_snake(k), TelemetryObject(json.loads(v), k))
         else:
             setattr(self, camel_to_snake(k), json.loads(v))
    else:
        setattr(self, camel_to_snake(k), v)
```

Specifically with `TelemetryObject(json.loads(v), k)`  on the condition when `if len(v) > 2` ( On Xbox it appears to always be filled, with every attribute set to 0? )

the `json.loads(v)` returns a list of dicts in this case instead of just a dict. Which then fails on the `__init__` of the TelemetryObject since it calls `items()` which doesn't exist on a list.


Unfortunately every test I do using PC  `v` is always equal to `[]` so it never gets to this point, so I am unsure if it is just an Xbox issue, or if a PC match with those values set would also cause problems as well.


 

As an unrelated find I made, which I can just create a pull request for if you'd like (now that I know how) is in:

Telemetry\telemetry.py

I added `from chicken_dinner.constants import map_to_map_name` since you use that mapping but it fails if I don't have this included.

issue_title: Parglare LR parser silently ignores input
Passing data to class-based djangp-jchart views
issue_body:
Is there a way to pass custom data to the views instead of hard-coding it?
The examples in this repo have hardcoded data like:
```
from jchart import Chart
from jchart.config import Axes, DataSet, rgba


class TimeSeriesChart(Chart):
    chart_type = 'line'

...
    def get_datasets(self, *args, **kwargs):
        data = [{'y': 0, 'x': '2017-01-02T00:00:00'}, {'y': 1, 'x': '2017-01-03T00:00:00'}, {'y': 4, 'x': '2017-01-04T00:00:00'}, {'y': 9, 'x': '2017-01-05T00:00:00'}, ...
        return [{
            'label': "My Dataset",
            'data': kwargs['data']
        }]
```

But I can't seem to overwrite get_datasets() to accept data from other scripts, eg. this won't work and will load empty data:
```
#views.py
class MainView(ListView, FormMixin):
    template_name = "src/template.html"
    model = DataClass

        lc = LineChart()
        lc.get_datasets(data=[loc.latitude for loc in locations_dis])
        context['line_chart'] = lc#data=[loc.latitude for loc in locations_dis])

        return context

class LineChart(Chart):
    chart_type = 'line'

    def get_datasets(self, **kwargs):
        try:
            data = kwargs['data']
        except KeyError:
            data = []
        return [{
            'label': "My Dataset",
            'data': data
        }]
```

Similarly, adding a __ init() __ will mess things up.

What is the `django-jchart` way to pass data do a view?

This gets more confusing when trying to implement the async loading. As per the doc, we should  add in the `url.py`:

```
from src.views import LineChart

urlpatterns = [
    url(r'^src/', include('src.urls')),
    url(r'^admin/', admin.site.urls),
    url(r'^charts/line_chart/$', ChartView.from_chart(LineChart()), name='line_chart'),
]

```
but how to pass the data to `LineChart()`?

issue_title: Use `request.full_path` instead of `request.path`
issue_body:
I have some problem with `flask.request.path` in [this line](https://github.com/cameronbwhite/Flask-CAS/blob/10ee70466ac9e71cec3602c1cd46f0566618f67e/flask_cas/__init__.py#L99)
Just like the way in [flask-login](https://github.com/maxcountryman/flask-login/blob/486c456907e307e363d0c334fb2c079d4fa25d55/flask_login/login_manager.py#L172): **flask-login** use the `request.url` which has the `query_string` in the path (THE **full_path**).

So, I propose to user `request.full_path` instead of `request.path`. Just like this:

```python
 flask.session['CAS_AFTER_LOGIN_SESSION_URL'] = flask.request.full_path
```

### Maybe, we should supply an option:  supply the `request.url` to **CAS Server** when redirect or just maintain the status quo.

issue_title: OrderedDict problem with Python 3.5
issue_body:
When using cms-cascade with Python 3.5, the `SegmentPlugin.get_form` call fails due to return type changes in `OrderedDict.values`, which does not return a list anymore but instead an odict_values object. This object does not support index lookups anymore and thus needs to be converted to a list before accessing its items by index.

The incompatible lines are here: https://github.com/jrief/djangocms-cascade/blob/master/cmsplugin_cascade/segmentation/cms_plugins.py#L135-L136

I submitted a pull request with a fix here: https://github.com/jrief/djangocms-cascade/pull/206

  Used in a referenced section. Forces the (sub)schema to be displayed as a separate schema.
  The value is used as the title of the (sub)schema and can be used when referencingthe (sub)schema.

- "$ref" : "schema reference"
  The reference is replaced with a clickable link to the referenced (sub schema).

issue_title: Multiple elements of the same name?
issue_body:
I need to generate some XML from a Python dictionary that has multiple elements of the same name.  

    <country>
        <city>Chicago</city>
        <city>Boston</city>
    </country>


Obviously the following won't work. I assumed it would produce a syntax error, but it simply hides one of the keys:

`dicttoxml({'country':{'city':'Chicago', 'city': 'Boston'}}, attr_type=False, root=False)`
 `'<country><city>Boston</city></country>'`

I tried a list, but that wraps each list element in an `item` element:

`dicttoxml({ 'country': [ {'city': 'Chicago'}, {'city': 'Boston'} ] }, attr_type=False, root=False)`
`'<country><item><city>Chicago</city></item><item><city>Boston</city></item></country>'`

Here's what I want the output to be

`'<country><city>Chicago</city><city>Boston</city></country>'`

What dictionary and/or function call settings (e.g. `item_func=None`) would produce that?

`CSV_DATE_INPUT_FORMATS = DATE_INPUT_FORMATS + ('%d-%m-%Y', '%Y-%m-%d')`

In Django 1.9, DATE_INPUT_FORMATS is a list, so this operation is invalid.

issue_title: Can't use proxy models with django-reversion
issue_body:
Hello!

Currently exception is thrown when one tries to register a proxy model. Advice is to register a parent class.

But django emits `post_save` signal with a proxy model class as a sender (*not a parent class*) so `_post_save_receiver` is omitted and thus a version/revision is lost.

Why exactly we may want to restrict proxy-model registration?

It works for me if I register both parent and proxy-child (assuming that I've commented out `raise RegistrationError(..)`).

issue_title: Async image processing - storage not serializable
issue_body:
I tried to process images asynchronously with Celery, followed the example and encountered an error:

`Exception Type: EncodeError`
`Exception Value: <django.core.files.storage.DefaultStorage object at 0x7f3c9052cf28> is not JSON serializable
`

The fix I found was not to pass storage to the task function:
```python
def image_processor(file_name, variations, storage):
    process_photo_image.delay(file_name, variations)
    return False  # prevent default rendering
```
and the task itself looks like:
```python
@shared_task
def process_photo_image(file_name, variations):
    render_variations(file_name, variations, replace=True)
```
Now it works fine, but there are two issues with that solution. Firstly, it's not possible to use other storage than the default one, since you can't pass it to the 'task function' as an argument. Secondly, I don't like the unused parameter in image_processor function.

I couldn't come up with any reasonable fix myself, so I open an issue.

#### Environment
Python 3.6.2
django==1.11.7
celery==4.1.0
django-stdimage==2.4.2

issue_title: EncryptedType does not handle unicode strings with chars >127
issue_body:
The EncrptedType doesn't properly handle unicode strings which include characters outside of ASCII encoding.

https://github.com/kvesteri/sqlalchemy-utils/blob/3b34e4a8e06d1818ab55c81365c886ac1d473643/sqlalchemy_utils/types/encrypted/encrypted_type.py#L89

Line 90: `value = str(value)` will result in UnicodeEncodeError 

Example program adapted from own example:

Key difference: `user_name = unichr(40960) + u'abcd' + unichr(1972)`

```
import sqlalchemy as sa
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy_utils import EncryptedType
from sqlalchemy_utils.types.encrypted.encrypted_type import AesEngine
secret_key = 'secretkey1234'
# setup
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///:memory:')
connection = engine.connect()
Base = declarative_base()


class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "user"
    id = sa.Column(sa.Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = sa.Column(EncryptedType(sa.Unicode,
                                       secret_key,
                                       AesEngine,
                                       'pkcs5'))


sa.orm.configure_mappers()
Base.metadata.create_all(connection)

# create a configured "Session" class
Session = sessionmaker(bind=connection)

# create a Session
session = Session()

# example

user_name = unichr(40960) + u'abcd' + unichr(1972)
user = User(username=user_name)

session.add(user)
session.commit()
user_id = user.id
session.expunge_all()
user_instance = session.query(User).get(user_id)
print('id: {}'.format(user_instance.id))
print('username: {}'.format(user_instance.username))

# teardown
session.close_all()
Base.metadata.drop_all(connection)
connection.close()
engine.dispose()
```

When you commit the session, the following error:

```
File "/Users/henry131/.virtualenvs/backend/lib/python2.7/site-packages/sqlalchemy_utils/types/encrypted/encrypted_type.py", line 90, in encrypt
    value = str(value)
sqlalchemy.exc.StatementError: (exceptions.UnicodeEncodeError) 'ascii' codec can't encode character u'\ua000' in position 0: ordinal not in range(128) [SQL: u'INSERT INTO user (username) VALUES (?)'] [parameters: [{'username': u'\ua000abcd\u07b4'}]]
```

issue_title: Issues in getting ListBox to Update
issue_body:
I've been trying to figure this out for a few days and have hit a wall. I'm having some strange issues in getting the ListBox to update and I have a feeling it's a weird behavior between asyncio and asciimatics. I have the below code example which basically has a listbox just displaying a single line - calling the print_gui function should update the last_status global variable and (I believe) update the listbox.

You can see that print_gui stops working once it's called inside of an async function. Not sure why though.
```
import asyncio

from asciimatics.widgets import Frame, ListBox, MultiColumnListBox, Layout, Divider, Text, \
    Button, TextBox, Widget
from asciimatics.scene import Scene
from asciimatics.screen import Screen
from asciimatics.exceptions import ResizeScreenError, NextScene, StopApplication

Screen_refresh_rate = 0.7


class ListView(Frame):
    def __init__(self, screen,model):
        super(ListView, self).__init__(screen,
                                       screen.height * 3 // 3,
                                       screen.width * 3 // 3,
                                       on_load=self._reload_list,
                                       hover_focus=True,
                                       title="StatusList")
        self._model =  model
        self._list_view = ListBox(
            Widget.FILL_FRAME,
            self._model,
            name="messages",
            on_change=None)
        layout = Layout([100], fill_frame=True)
        self.add_layout(layout)
        layout.add_widget(self._list_view)
        layout.add_widget(Divider())
        layout3 = Layout([1, 1])
        self.add_layout(layout3)
        layout3.add_widget(Button("Quit", self._quit), 1)
        self.fix()
        self._on_pick()

    def _on_pick(self):
        pass

    def _reload_list(self, new_value=None):
        self._list_view.options = self._model
        self._list_view.value = new_value

    def _close(self):
        self.save()
        self._reload_list()

    @staticmethod
    def _quit():
        raise StopApplication("User pressed quit")
    
    
def print_gui(string):
    global last_status
    last_status = [(string,1)]
   
async def draw_screen():
    global last_status
    screen = Screen.open()
    scenes = [
        Scene([ListView(screen,last_status)], -1, name="Main"),
    ]  
    try:
        screen.set_scenes(scenes)
        #
        while True:
            screen.force_update()
            screen.draw_next_frame(repeat=False)
            await asyncio.sleep(Screen_refresh_rate)
    except Exception as e:
        print_gui(e)
    finally:
        screen.close(True)

async def test_print():
    while True:
        global last_status
        print_gui('This doesnt work')
        await asyncio.sleep(5)


#Current string that should be printed to screen
last_status = [('init',1)]

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()

#setup background task to update the screen with asciimatics
loop.create_task(draw_screen())

#verify printing to screen works as intended
print_gui('This works')

#Trying to do it within an async function doesn't work....
loop.create_task(test_print())

loop.run_forever()
loop.close()
```

issue_title: expected_cumulative_transactions will calculate actual transactions incorrectly
issue_body:
While I am using plot_cumulative_transactions, I think I found a bug in utils.expected_cumulative_transactions.

Let's say I want to build a beta_geo_fitter model, and freq='D'.  First I will call utils.summary_data_from_transaction_data to summarize my transaction.  That function will treat transactions which happened on the **same** day as one transaction when it calculates _frequency_.  

Later on, when I call plot_cumulative_transactions, I see that it calls utils.expected_cumulative_transactions, but that function will treat multiple transactions happening on the same day as multiple transactions (see the line below).

`act_transactions = (transactions_current.groupby(customer_id_col).size() - 1).sum()`

As a result, the actual and expected lines I got from plot_cumulative_transactions look very different.  I worked around this by calling df.drop_duplicates([customer_id_col, date_col]) on my transaction dataframe first before I call plot_cumulative_transactions.

issue_title: TRACKABLE info is not stored when using login_user directly
issue_body:
We use
```
from flask_security import login_user
@app.route('/custom_login', methods=['POST'])
def custom_login():
    login_user(some_user)
    return redirect(...)
```
and noticed that information regarding current_login, last login, etc is not stored into the DB (sqlalchemy for us) unless we add a `db.session.commit()` after the `login_user(...)` call. 

After reading a bit the code, it looks like it could be fixed by adding a call to `after_this_request(db.session.commit)` in the `flask_securtity.utils.login_user` method. Not sure where we should add a unit test for this. Please if @mattupstate can hint us a bit on where is a good place to write the test for it, it would be great help to submit a PR and save you some work.

Thanks a lot.

issue_title: Update the client and API to take advantage of the TestRail `get_results_for_run` endpoint
issue_body:
The `testrail-python` code currently only utilizes one of the available end points for collecting results:
(from client.py)
```
    # Result Methods
    def results(self, test_id):
        return map(Result, self.api.results(test_id))
```

(from api.py)
```
    # Result Requests
    def results(self, test_id):
        if self._refresh(self._results[test_id]['ts']):
            _results = self._get('get_results/%s' % test_id)
            self._results[test_id]['value'] = _results
            self._results[test_id]['ts'] = datetime.now()
        return self._results[test_id]['value']
```

This is very inefficient if you are trying to collect all results across a series of runs. TestRail offers a `get_results_for_run` endpoint that can return bulk results per run:
http://docs.gurock.com/testrail-api2/reference-results#get_results_for_run

Update the client and API to accept either a test or run, and return results accordingly.

This fails here:

issue_title: get_historical did not return the range specified. Did I missed anything?
The right way to know if the profile is expired is to look at date_joined?
issue_body:
Hello!
First I'll like to thank you guys for the support for this really helpful app!

So, my question is this:
Lets say if a user that already have a Login go to my site and he decides to change his email. So, in my point of view, it is needed to resend an activation email again, to verify if this email is valid.
So, I set the user to inactive (user.is_active=False) and call the resend_activation_mail.
The user will go to his inbox, click on the link that I sent and activate his user again.

This workflow seems coherent, right?
But is not. If you see the code for the activate_user it only active a user that is not expired. And to know if the RegistrationProfile is expired it looks based on the date_joined field, that can be months ago! So that user will no longer be able to active his profile.

An other problem that I see that can happen is, If Model User have the email field defined as unique=True and the user don't have a login yet:
The user fill his profile to create a new profile with a email that he is not the owner, so, the user will never be activate.

If another user try to create a profile with this same email it won't be able.
So a user can disable a email forever, even if the user is not the owner of the email address.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
What are your suggestions to resolve this?
Maybe could have a field in the RegistrationProfile, like date_created. And the is_expired method look for this field instead of the date_joined of the user.
I will be thankful to help!

issue_title: create_new_activation_key is insecure
issue_body:
It uses the python `random.random` which is not cryptographically secure and also unnecessarily uses the user's ID in the hash which makes it possible (although improbable) for the same key to be generated for a different user if their IDs overlap. For example, a user is created with an ID, then at some point deleted and a new user is created with the same ID.

I suggest using an algorithm that uses a secure random function to create an entirely random string or at least allow the user to specify their own algorithm.

Such an algorithm may look like this:

```
def create_new_activation_key(self, save=True):
        """
        Create a new activation key for the user
        """
        self.activation_key = hashlib.sha1(os.urandom(32)).hexdigest()
        if save:
            self.save()
        return self.activation_key
```

I am happy to make a PR if this is something you will consider merging?

issue_title: cassandra.cqlengine.CQLEngineException: Connection name '<object object at 0x7eff59dcc8e0>' doesn't exist in the registry.

issue_title: incorrect join types for nullable translated relations with an OR Q query
issue_body:
Assume:
I have a database that keeps track of movies and tv shows
Movies and tv shows are stored in the same table.
movies have genres, (M2M field) and tv shows have a relation to Series which have genres (M2M field)
Genre's are translatable, but VideoAsset isn't

I want to filter to assets that belong to a given genre.

`genre_query = Q(genres__in=list_genres) | Q(series__genres__in=list_genres)`

`VideoAssets.objects.filter(genre_query)`
will generate a query that uses LEFT JOIN to join the two genre tables.

`get_translation_aware_manager(VideoAssets).filter(genre_query)` on the other hand will use INNER JOIN's and as a result no results would be obtained.

issue_title: Running 32-bit Python on x64 Windows identifies the OS architecture incorrectly
issue_body:
Hi Sergey,

To identify the OS bitness you've used `platform.architecture()` call, but it returns an architecture of Python itself. Rather than the OS. 
Cases when I have 32-bit Python on Win64 machines:
1. After the recent release a 64-bit version of Python 3.6 for Windows was not installable for a while;
In stdout when running the webdriver_manager:
```
...
Checking for win32 geckodriver:v0.19.1 in cache   # Win32 version got downloaded.
```
Python cli:
```
>>> platform.architecture()
('32bit', 'WindowsPE')
>>> platform.machine()
'AMD64'
```
2. I run Conda virtual environments on another Win10.
In stdout when running the webdriver_manager:
```
...
Checking for win geckodriver:v0.19.1 in cache   # Win32 version got downloaded.
```
Note the architecture is just 'win' for some reason.
Though in Python cli:
```
>>> platform.architecture()
('64bit', 'WindowsPE')
>>> platform.machine()
'AMD64'
```

It seems like `platform.machine()` call returns more relevant data. But there likely are some other possible options.

I'm here to answer any other questions one might have.

issue_title: Task headers are None when using Task.retry()
issue_body:
When using Task.retry( ) with the `fix_missing_headers` branch (Issue #12) the `headers` key in the kwargs is None.

This causes `self._add_current_schema(kw.setdefault('headers', {}))` in https://github.com/maciej-gol/tenant-schemas-celery/blob/fix_missing_headers/tenant_schemas_celery/task.py#L22 to return None rather than an empty dict.

https://github.com/maciej-gol/tenant-schemas-celery/blob/fix_missing_headers/tenant_schemas_celery/task.py#L13 will the Throw `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'setdefault'` 

We're using Celery 4.0.2 with redis and https://github.com/maciej-gol/tenant-schemas-celery/tree/fix_missing_headers.

I've opened PR #13 that has some small changes to make sure  that headers is a dict before attempting to add the schema name to it.

issue_title: Stop copying system tests to bin/
issue_body:
We copy system tests at build time, from:

    src/niswitch/system_tests/test_system_niswitch.py

to:

    bin/niswitch/system_tests/test_system_niswitch.py

If memory serves me right, we do this for historical reasons: we used to play import path tricks rather than install the modules so we needed the tests to be in a specific location relative to the built modules. This is no longer true - now we install the modules in the virtual environment. So we should be able to stop copying the files and run them from their location in src/.
